things.name: Tower of Hercules
things.description: The world's only working Roman lighthouse in continuous use since the 2nd century AD; a UNESCO World Heritage site with 242 steps to ocean panoramas

things.name: Old Town (Ciudad Vieja)
things.description: A Coruna's medieval old town on the promontory with the collegiate Church of Santa Maria del Campo, Romanesque architecture and a charming maze of lanes

things.name: Crystal Galleries (Galerias de Cristal)
things.description: The city's iconic enclosed glass balconies lining the seafront promenade; a unique architectural feature that gave A Coruna the nickname Crystal City

things.name: Aquarium Finisterrae
things.description: Award-winning aquarium exploring the marine biodiversity of Galicia's wild Atlantic coast, with impressive tanks of local sea life including conger eels and rays

things.name: Museum of Fine Arts
things.description: Galicia's oldest art museum with an impressive collection of Spanish art from the 16th century onwards, including notable works by Goya and Rubens

things.name: Orzan Beach and Riazor Beach
things.description: Two fine city beaches with Atlantic surf backed by the seafront promenade; Riazor hosts a section of the famous Camino Ingles pilgrimage route

things.name: Domus (Man Museum)
things.description: Arata Isozaki-designed interactive museum exploring human biology, genetics and the senses; a bold sail-shaped landmark on the seafront promenade

things.name: Monte de San Pedro Park
things.description: Hilltop park with a glass-domed pergola offering 360-degree panoramas over the estuary, city and Atlantic Ocean; reached by a free outdoor lift

things.name: Camino Ingles (English Way)
things.description: The starting point of the English Way pilgrimage to Santiago de Compostela; medieval pilgrims from Britain and Northern Europe landed here to begin their walk

things.name: Estadio Abanca-Riazor
things.description: Home of RC Deportivo de La Coruna; scene of the club's legendary 4-0 defeat of AC Milan in the 2004 Champions League quarter-final comeback
